mnemonics: S.P.A.R.K. your text analysis: Spark Processes All Records Kindly (in-memory and fast)
keyPoints: Spark is a distributed computing framework designed for fast, in-memory data processing.
It excels at handling large-scale text data through its Resilient Distributed Dataset (RDD) and DataFrame APIs.
Core operations for text analysis include reading text files, transformations (like map, filter), and actions (like count, collect).
commonMistakes: Forgetting that Spark transformations are lazy; they only execute when an action is called.
Misunderstanding the difference between a transformation (returns a new RDD/DataFrame) and an action (returns a value to the driver).
Trying to collect() a very large dataset to the driver node, which can cause out-of-memory errors.
